volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 2.4.490b - Section 3.1.540b)

sections.label: Section 2.4.490b
sections.translation: Throw in something by way of surplus ; even a thousand didrachms more than he shall demand .

sections.label: Section 2.4.491b
sections.translation: Now , do hold your peace . But what say you as to this ? Whence will the money come , for you to give , when your father asks for it ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.493
sections.translation: It shall be found , it shall be sought out , something shall be done .

sections.label: Section 2.4.494
sections.translation: You are worrying me to death . For I ’ m afraid of that Something shall be done .

sections.label: Section 2.4.494b
sections.translation: Why won ’ t you hold your tongue ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.494c
sections.translation: You give your commands to one who is dumb .

sections.label: Section 2.4.495
sections.translation: Is this matter sufficiently pointed out to you ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.495b
sections.translation: Can ’ t you possibly be attending to something else ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.495c
sections.translation: It isn ’ t possible .

sections.label: Section 2.4.496
sections.translation: Kindly fare you well .

sections.label: Section 2.4.496b
sections.original: Non edepol possum prius quam tu ad me redieris .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I cannot fare well , before you come back to me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.497
sections.translation: To better purpose , recover your senses .

sections.label: Section 2.4.497b
sections.translation: Farewell , and prevail , and be my preserver .

sections.label: Section 2.4.497c
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so . Wait for me at home .

sections.label: Section 2.4.498b
sections.original: Ergo actutum face cum praeda recipias .—
sections.translation: Do you take care , then , to betake yourself back just now with the booty .

sections.label: Section 3.1.499
sections.original: Amíce amico operam dedi : vicinus quod rogavit ,
sections.translation: I ’ ve lent my assistance to my friend in a friendly manner ; this piece of goods , which my neighbour requested me , I ’ ve purchased . ( Turning to PASICOMPSA .) You are my own ; then follow me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.501
sections.original: ne plora : nimis stulte facis , oculos corrumpis tales .
sections.translation: Don ’ t weep . You are acting very foolishly ; spoiling such eyes . Why , really you have more reason to laugh than to be crying .

sections.label: Section 3.1.503
sections.translation: In the name of heaven , prithee , my good old gentleman , do tell me —

sections.label: Section 3.1.503b
sections.translation: Ask me what you please .

sections.label: Section 3.1.504
sections.translation: Why have you bought me ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.504b
sections.original: Tene ego ? ut quod imperetur facias ,
sections.translation: What , I , bought you ? For you to do what you are bidden ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.505
sections.original: item quod tu mihi si imperes , ego faciam .
sections.translation: in like manner what you bid me , I ’ ll do .

sections.label: Section 3.1.505b
sections.translation: I am determined , to the best of my ability and skill , to do what I shall think you desire .

sections.label: Section 3.1.507
sections.original: Laboriosi nil tibi quicquam operis imperabo .
sections.translation: I shall bid you do nothing of laborious work .

sections.label: Section 3.1.508
sections.original: Namque edepol equidem , mi senex , non didici baiolare
sections.translation: Why , really , for my part , my good old gentleman , I haven ’ t learnt , i ’ faith , to carry burdens , or to feed cattle at the farm , or to nurse children .

sections.label: Section 3.1.510
sections.original: Bona si esse vis , bene erit tibi .
sections.translation: If you choose to be a good girl , it shall be well for you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.510b
sections.translation: Then , i ’ faith , to my sorrow , I ’ m undone .

sections.label: Section 3.1.511
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.511b
sections.original: Quia illim unde huc advecta sum , malis bene esse solitumst .
sections.translation: Because in the place from which I have been conveyed hither , it used to be well with the worthless [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.1.512
sections.original: Quasi dicas nullam mulierem bonam esse .
sections.translation: As though you would say that no woman is good .

sections.label: Section 3.1.512b
sections.translation: Indeed I don ’ t say so ; nor is it my way , to say a thing which I believe all people are acquainted with .

sections.label: Section 3.1.514
sections.original: Oratio edepol pluris est huius quam quanti haec emptast .
sections.translation: By my troth , her talk alone is worth more than the sum that she was purchased at .

sections.label: Section 3.1.515
sections.translation: I want to ask this one thing of you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.515b
sections.translation: I ’ ll answer you when you ask .

sections.label: Section 3.1.516
sections.original: Quid ais tu ? quid nomen tibi dicam esse ?
sections.translation: What say you now ? What am I to say your name is ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.516b
sections.translation: Pasicompsa .

sections.label: Section 3.1.517
sections.original: Ex forma nomen inditumst . sed quid ais , Pasicompsa ?
sections.translation: The name was given you from your good looks [*] . But what say you , Pasicompsa ? Can you , if occasion should arise , spin a fine woof ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.519
sections.translation: I can .

sections.label: Section 3.1.519b
sections.original: Si tenue scis , scio te uberius posse nere .
sections.translation: If you know how to do a fine one , I ’ m sure you can spin a coarser one .

sections.label: Section 3.1.520
sections.original: De lanificio neminem metuo , una aetate quae sit .
sections.translation: For spinning , I fear no woman that ’ s of the same age .

sections.label: Section 3.1.521
sections.original: Bonam hercle te et frugi arbitror , matura iam † inde aetate
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I take it that you are good and industrious , since , young woman , now that you are grown up , you know how to do your duty .

sections.label: Section 3.1.522b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I learned it from a skilful mistress . I won ’ t let my work be called in question .

sections.label: Section 3.1.523b
sections.translation: Well , thus the matter stands , i ’ faith . Look now , I ’ ll give you a sheep for your own , one sixty years old .

sections.label: Section 3.1.525b
sections.translation: My good old gentleman , one so old as that ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.525c
sections.translation: It ’ s of the Grecian breed . If you take care of it , it is a very good one ; it is shorn very easily .

sections.label: Section 3.1.527
sections.original: Honoris causa quidquid est quod dabitur gratum habebo .
sections.translation: For the sake of the compliment , whatever it is that shall be given me , I shall receive it with thanks .

sections.label: Section 3.1.528
sections.original: Nunc , mulier , ne tu frustra sis , mea non es , ne arbitrere .
sections.translation: Now , damsel , that you mayn ’ t be mistaken , you are not mine ; so don ’ t think it .

sections.label: Section 3.1.529
sections.translation: Prithee , tell me , then , whose I am ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.529b
sections.translation: You ’ ve been bought back for your own master .

sections.label: Section 3.1.530
sections.original: ego te redemi , ílle mecum oravit .
sections.translation: I ’ ve bought you back for him [*] ; he requested me to do so .

sections.label: Section 3.1.530b
sections.translation: My spirits have returned , if good faith is kept with me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.531b
sections.translation: Be of good courage ; this person will give you your liberty . I ’ troth , he did so dote upon you this day as soon as ever he had seen you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.533
sections.original: Ecastor iam bienniumst , quom mecum rem coëpit .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , it ’ s now two years since he commenced his connexion with me . Now , as I ’ m sure that you are a friend of his , I ’ ll disclose it .

sections.label: Section 3.1.535
sections.original: Quid ais tu ? iam bienniumst , quom tecum rém habet ?
sections.translation: How say you ? Is it now two years since he formed the connexion with you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.535b
sections.translation: Certainly , it is ; and we agreed , on oath , between ourselves , I with him , and he with me , that I would never have intercourse with any man except himself , nor he with any woman except myself .

sections.label: Section 3.1.537b
sections.translation: Immortal Gods ! Isn ’ t he even to sleep with his wife ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.538b
sections.translation: Prithee , is he a married man ? He neither is nor will he be .

sections.label: Section 3.1.539b
sections.translation: Indeed , I wish he wasn ’ t . I ’ faith , the fellow has been committing perjury .

sections.label: Section 3.1.540
sections.translation: No young man do I more ardently love .

sections.label: Section 3.1.540b
sections.translation: Why , really he ’ s a child , you simpleton ; for , in fact , it ’ s not so very long a time since his teeth fell out .
